exemplar.title: What music genres (fast-tempo (170-190 bpm), medium-tempo (130-150 bpm), slow-tempo (80-110 bpm), motivational, relaxing, no-music) most effectively enhance sports performance during touch rugby training?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

detailedExemplarData.title: What music genres (fast-tempo (170-190 bpm), medium-tempo (130-150 bpm), slow-tempo (80-110 bpm), motivational, relaxing, no-music) most effectively enhance sports performance during touch rugby training?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Topic and research question are communicated accurately and consistently, with clear purpose and focus throughout.
Research question is explicit, well-scoped, and effectively anchors the structure of the essay.
Application section translates findings into actionable strategies, demonstrating strong real-world connection.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks justification for source selection and omits criteria for assessing source quality.
No explanation of how quantitative data were compared or how BPM ranges were trimmed.
Implementation details (e.g., session timing, playlist curation) are missing, reducing methodological authenticity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Topic communication and research question formulation clearly meet the 5â6 descriptors, showing accurate, effective focus. However, the methodology strand sits at the 3â4 band due to limited justification and absence of source-quality criteria. On balance, a holistic score of 5 reflects the strong focus and RQ alongside the methodological shortcomings.

strengths: Good summarisation and application of physiological and psychological concepts (energy systems, fatigue, RPE, lactate) to the research question.
Solid integration of brain-region and neural-pathway discussions demonstrating command of specialized concepts.
Personal narrative effectively contextualises the investigation within the studentâs touch rugby experience.
weaknesses: Occasional inaccuracies in cortisol description and confusion over heart-rate findings.
Spelling and grammar lapses (e.g., âfatiqueâ) and missing full statistical reporting (degrees of freedom, F-statistics).
Specialist terms (e.g., theta wave activity, bilateral activation) introduced without definition, reducing clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Subject knowledge is good and mostly relevant (band 3â4 top end), but factual slips and inconsistent term definitions prevent an âexcellentâ (5â6) rating. Use of terminology is adequate but not always precise. A holistic score of 4 accurately reflects these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Accurate interpretation of p-values for tempo effects, clearly linked to endurance performance.
Well-structured synthesis comparing endurance and high-intensity exercise findings tied back to the research question.
Evaluation section identifies sample-size and demographic limitations, showing good critical awareness.
weaknesses: Critical evaluation is confined to a separate section rather than embedded throughout.
Argument development is often descriptive with limited counter-argument or evidence weighting.
Superficial treatment of statistical significance and effect sizes reduces depth of analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Critical Thinking strands sit within the 4â6 band: partially critical evaluation, good analysis, and coherent but moderately deep argumentation. Awarding 6 marks reflects performance at the top of this bandâsolid critical work tempered by a lack of sustained, integrated critique and deeper synthesis.

strengths: Consistent application of font, spacing, in-text citations, and reference list; word count and section requirements are met.
Table of contents follows IB conventions, providing clear navigation.
Appendix and headings (e.g., tempo section) aid reader orientation and academic rigor.
weaknesses: Minor pagination glitches and inconsistent DOI/hyperlink formatting.
Typos and terminology slips (e.g., âtough rugbyâ) interrupt professionalism.
Densely packed appendix table reduces legibility; some mis-labelled headings and punctuation inconsistencies.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation adheres to IB conventions with only minor errors. Structure and formatting are appropriate, meriting the top of the 3â4 band. A score of 4 reflects generally strong presentation with room for refinement in consistency and clarity.

strandSelections.reasoning: A separate âEvaluationâ section recognises sample-size bias, participant homogeneity and variability in music preference, showing some critical insight.  However, evaluation is brief, not integrated throughout, and does not reflect on methodology of the studentâs own approach.  This meets the descriptor âpartially critical evaluationâ, aligning with 4-6, bottom end.

The research question â "What music genres (fast-tempo â¦) most effectively enhance sports performance during touch rugby training?" â is explicit, sharply delimited to tempo/genre and three performance metrics, and it drives the structure of each section (no-music vs music, tempo, genre, application).  References and discussion remain tied to that question, so focus is strong and consistent, justifying placement in the top band.

Font, spacing, in-text citations and reference list are consistently applied; word count is stated and under the limit.  A few hyperlink breaks and inconsistent DOI formatting are present, yet the general presentation is appropriate and reader-friendly, supporting a 3-4 band placement (score 3).

The report contains all required EE sections, a table of contents with page references, clear headings and an appendix.  Minor pagination glitches and some mis-labelled headings occur, but overall structure adheres to IB conventions, earning the 3-4 band (score 3).

The title, research question and purpose are all presented on the cover page; the introduction immediately explains the personal context, key variables (tempo ranges, genres), chosen sport and performance indicators.  Throughout the essay the student keeps referring back to âtouch-rugby trainingâ, showing a clear and consistent focus.  Minor wording slips (âtough rugbyâ, etc.) do not impede understanding.  Overall the topic is communicated accurately and with a clear sense of direction, matching the 5-6 descriptor.

The student summarises physiological and psychological concepts (energy systems, fatigue, RPE, lactate) correctly and applies them to the findings of each study and to touch-rugby demands.  Occasional inaccuracies (e.g., cortisol description, confusion over HR findings) prevent a truly âexcellentâ standard, but overall knowledge is good and mostly relevant (3-4 band, top end).

Specialist terms such as BPM, ergogenic, autonomic nervous system, lactate accumulation and Borg RPE scale are used correctly in most cases.  However, spelling/grammar lapses and a few mis-quotes of statistical results show some inconsistency, so the strand sits solidly in the âadequate / mostly accurateâ range (score 3).

Analysis mainly consists of comparing study outcomes (HR, RPE, power output) with and without music, and linking these to touch rugby needs.  The connections are relevant and generally sound, but treatment of statistical significance and mechanisms is rather superficial and there is little synthesis across studies.  This reflects a âgoodâ but not âexcellentâ level, warranting the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The methodology is described in a short bullet list and centres on secondary research only.  A reasonable range of peer-reviewed articles is consulted and organised in a spreadsheet (appendix shown), but there is little justification for inclusion/exclusion, no comment on source quality, and no explanation of how quantitative data were compared.  Thus the approach is âmostly completeâ with âsome evidence of informed selectionâ, corresponding to 3-4.

The essay follows a logical sequence (no-music vs music â tempo â genre â application) and each section ends with a linking sentence.  Nevertheless, argumentation is often descriptive; counter-arguments and weighting of evidence are thin.  Coherence is clear but depth is moderate, hence a mid-band 4.

annotations.comment: Interpretation of p-values for tempo effects is accurate and clearly explained in context of endurance performance.
Discuss cortisol level trends beyond five minutes post-exercise to provide a fuller picture of relaxation music effects.
Paragraph breaks within the genres discussion appear abruptly; consider refining sentences for smoother narrative flow.
The application section translates findings into actionable strategies for touch rugby, demonstrating strong connection to real-world training.
The final summary effectively highlights both physiological and psychological mechanisms by which music enhances performance.
The title clearly encapsulates the investigationâs focus on music and touch rugby training, providing immediate clarity of purpose and direction.
The research question is explicit, well scoped to tempo and genre variables, and effectively anchors the structure of the essay.
Methodology outlines clear steps but lacks justification for selecting secondary research and omits criteria for source quality.
Typo in methodology step 4 (âtough rugbyâ); maintain consistency of terminology to uphold professionalism.
Word count is clearly stated and under the 4000-word limit, demonstrating compliance with IB formatting requirements.
The table of contents follows IB conventions and provides clear navigation of sections, aiding reader orientation.
Section headings in the table of contents slightly differ from in-text headings; ensure exact consistency to avoid confusion.
The personal narrative effectively establishes motivation and contextualizes the research within the studentâs touch rugby experience.
Inconsistent punctuation (e.g., stray period after âOver the past 3 years.â) interrupts flow; revise sentence structure for clarity.
Excellent integration of aerobic and anaerobic concepts to justify the investigationâs focus on endurance versus high-intensity components of touch rugby.
Justification for trimming standard BPM ranges (e.g., reducing Allegro) is limited; explain rationale for your chosen categories.
The discussion of mental fatigue and neurotransmitter involvement demonstrates solid subject knowledge and connection to the research question.
Spelling error (âfatiqueâ) and vague phrasing when defining fatigue types; correct terminology and refine definitions.
The tempo section heading signals a clear shift in focus to BPM analysis, aiding reader orientation.
Expand on effect sizes and statistical significance when analyzing HR variability study to deepen the critical evaluation.
Citation formatting error in â(R) Archana and R Mukilan)â; revise in-text reference style for consistency.
The synthesis comparing endurance and high-intensity exercise findings is well structured and tied back to the research question.
Convert inline math to LaTeX (e.g., $$4.1 \pm 3.6$$) to standardize equation formatting and improve readability.
The open question at the end reflects genuine intellectual curiosity; consider proposing a pilot study to explore recovery mechanisms further.
Heading level is inconsistent (H1 among H2s); standardize tag hierarchy to maintain formatting consistency.
ANOVA results lack degrees of freedom and F-statistics; include full reporting (e.g., F(2,34)=5.67, p<0.01) for rigor.
Consider adding implementation details (e.g., session timing, playlist curation) to reinforce authenticity of training recommendations.
The conclusion reiterates key findings but duplicates earlier text; tighten and focus on synthesizing insights rather than restating.
Clarify earlier contradictory statements about cortisol to ensure conceptual coherence in discussion of hormonal mechanisms.
The detailed discussion of brain regions and neural pathways demonstrates excellent command of specialized concepts.
Terms like âtheta wave activityâ and âbilateral activationâ are introduced without definition; add brief explanations for reader clarity.
The evaluation section rightly identifies sample size and demographic limitations, showing good critical awareness.
Embed critical reflections throughout the essay rather than confining them to the evaluation section to demonstrate ongoing analytical engagement.
The work cited section is comprehensive with consistent DOI and access date formatting, supporting academic rigor.
Appendix table is densely packed with columns, reducing legibility; consider splitting into two tables or simplifying categories.
